description: FINN-WILSON. stakeholder graph. Sources: stakeholder map v1 (50 seats) + HeyReach inbox scan 2026-06-12 (24 contacts). Feeds market-insight-graph as person/relationship layer.

nodes.name: Delta Capacity
nodes.why: The capital behind Delta Charge: a Scandinavian BESS developer-investor backing a €300M deployment ambition in truck depots and battery hubs. Your shortest route from delivery to deal-making runs through this firm.
nodes.warm_path: Direct. You are inside their portfolio company's flagship German rollout.
nodes.first_move: Ask Delta Charge for the introduction through programme governance. Then ask to see how the SPV and revenue stack are built.

nodes.name: Chris Morrison
nodes.why: Runs the Macquarie-backed eHGV charging platform: Tilbury operational, Hams Hall consented, seven further sites with grid connections secured, construction pushing through 2026. The closest UK analogue to what FINN-WILSON. wants to become.
nodes.warm_path: Cold-warm: shared market, overlapping ecosystem, mutual reference points.
nodes.first_move: Build a comment trail on Fleete's site milestones with operator-grade observations. Then one direct note comparing depot and hub economics.

nodes.name: NZGIF
nodes.why: Put NZ$20M behind Zenobē's New Zealand entry. The single most important capital node for the landing: they fund exactly what you will be selling.
nodes.warm_path: The relocation story is the opener; the Zenobē ANZ work gives shared reference points.
nodes.first_move: Six months before the first NZ trip, one short note: European depot playbooks meeting NZ capital. Ask for 30 minutes.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: Zenobē's long-term institutional backer. Knows precisely how fleet electrification gets financed at platform scale — the education you want.
nodes.warm_path: Via Zenobē relationships or the London infrastructure circuit.
nodes.first_move: Identify the partner covering e-mobility on LinkedIn; follow their portfolio announcements before any approach.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: Dutch pension capital active in European charging financings. Amsterdam-based — coffee distance from your desk.
nodes.warm_path: Amsterdam energy and infra events; Prologis capital markets colleagues likely know the team.
nodes.first_move: Map the infrastructure team on LinkedIn; target the person covering transport and energy.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: Debt financier behind Zenobē's Leichhardt depot. Australia's clean energy bank sits inside most serious AU fleet deals.
nodes.warm_path: ARENA and CEFC publish knowledge-sharing material — engage publicly first.
nodes.first_move: Reference their depot financings in one published piece, then connect.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: The second Dutch pension giant appearing in European charging deals. Same logic as APG, one step further out.
nodes.warm_path: Same Dutch institutional circuit as APG.
nodes.first_move: Ambient follow; engage when they announce transport infrastructure positions.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: Lent CTP €200M for portfolio solar and acts as implementing partner for AFIF. The institutional money underneath European logistics energy.
nodes.warm_path: Policy and industry events. Long game.
nodes.first_move: Cite their lending in your AFIF and depot-charging commentary.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: The PE owner that co-funded TGE's A$44M Depot of the Future. Australian private capital that already believes in depot electrification.
nodes.warm_path: Through the Team Global Express programme contacts in the ANZ tier.
nodes.first_move: Approach the operating team only after the TGE relationship exists.

nodes.name: Prologis
nodes.why: Your sponsors and your first client. The exit-to-retainer play runs through these relationships — treat internal stakeholders as the most important accounts on the map.
nodes.first_move: Quarterly discipline: make sure DSV and BESS milestones are visible two levels up, in writing, with your name attached.

nodes.name: Prologis
nodes.why: Your education in the biggest power adjacency in the market: grid connections, procurement, behind-the-meter generation at 10–100x depot scale.
nodes.first_move: Ask for a standing monthly 30 minutes. You bring mobility load insight; they teach you data centre power.

nodes.name: Stefano Fissolo
nodes.why: Runs the closest competitor energy platform: onsite solar, battery storage and EV infrastructure across the GLP estate. Ex-SUSI Partners, so he speaks capital fluently. Peer today; client or partner later.
nodes.warm_path: Same conference circuit, overlapping vendors and counterparties.
nodes.first_move: Engage GLP Clean Energy's published work; meet at the next European logistics-energy event.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: Calls energy its third business unit: 400+ MWp solar push, EIB-financed, expanding into tenant charging and storage across CEE. Listed on Euronext Amsterdam — local capital-markets angle.
nodes.warm_path: Amsterdam investor circuit; CEE logistics events.
nodes.first_move: Identify the energy MD via LinkedIn. Open on CEE depot charging — nobody covers it well, and they own the ground.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: UK and EU big-box landlord with a maturing energy programme. A natural future buyer of the playbook you are writing at Prologis.
nodes.first_move: Map the team; engage their published energy targets with specifics, not praise.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: Pivoted hard into data centres — the same grid-and-power problem you are learning internally. A bridge between your two worlds, and a door to the ANZ market where Goodman is strongest.
nodes.warm_path: Data-centre power events; Prologis DC colleagues will know counterparts.
nodes.first_move: Connect after two quarters of DC learning are banked. Arrive credible.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: Mid-size landlord with its own renewables vehicle. Smaller and faster-moving — a realistic early consultancy client.
nodes.first_move: Ambient follow; approach once FINN-WILSON. is live.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: Huge development volume, less mature energy programme — which is exactly the opportunity.
nodes.first_move: Hold until launch. They are a prospect, not a peer.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: Last-mile portfolio means dense, small-depot charging demand. Different problem shape, same buyer profile. Amsterdam headquartered.
nodes.first_move: Ambient; one coffee in 2027.

nodes.name: Denis Sepoetro
nodes.why: Owns the product family your programme plugs into — electric road transport sits inside his remit in Group Sustainability Insights. The person who can institutionalise depot charging across DSV rather than approve it site by site.
nodes.warm_path: Live deal. Route through programme governance.
nodes.first_move: Get him into one DSV–Prologis working session this quarter.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: Lease expiry is your retention argument — this seat is where it lands. The commercial counterpart to the sustainability story.
nodes.first_move: Ask your account director to map DSV's property organisation; identify the portfolio decision-maker.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.role: Land transport decarbonisation lead, Schenker side
nodes.why: The April 2025 merger doubled the network. Schenker-side champions decide whether your programme scales across the combined estate or stalls at the legacy boundary.
nodes.warm_path: Through Denis Sepoetro's team as integration settles.
nodes.first_move: Ask in governance: who owns land-transport decarbonisation on the Schenker side now?

nodes.name: Jens Lund
nodes.why: Air cover. Post-Schenker, network-wide electrification is a CEO-level story. You do not contact him; you make sure the programme reaches his deck with your name on it.
nodes.first_move: Ambient. Track his public statements on decarbonisation for framing.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: The other giant 3PL. Everything proved at DSV becomes a reference case here within a fortnight of being public.
nodes.warm_path: Industry events; likely a Prologis tenant relationship somewhere in the portfolio.
nodes.first_move: Hold direct contact until a DSV milestone is public. Then one sharp note.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: The largest EV fleet programme in European logistics, and they publish enough that you can engage substantively from outside.
nodes.first_move: Comment trail on their electrification output; meet at Power2Drive or an eTruck event.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: A national e-fleet at scale, headquartered up the road. A friendly first audience for your thinking and a Dutch reference relationship.
nodes.first_move: One coffee this year. Low stakes, high local signal.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: Committed to a fully electric home-delivery fleet by 2030 and anchor client of Zenobē's Mascot truck charging hub — the most advanced fleet electrifier in your future market.
nodes.warm_path: Via Gareth Ridge and Zenobē ANZ, or ARENA knowledge-sharing events.
nodes.first_move: When the move firms up, ask Zenobē for the introduction. Arrive as the European operator, not a job-seeker.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.role: Depot of the Future programme lead
nodes.why: Ran Australia's largest fleet electrification trial: 60 BEVs, a 1MWh battery, A$20.1M of ARENA funding, learnings published. The best ANZ conversation available about what actually breaks in a depot.
nodes.warm_path: ARENA's knowledge bank names the team; LinkedIn from there.
nodes.first_move: Open with a specific question from their published learnings. Show you read them.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: New Zealand's flagship logistics company: global network, owns much of its property, long-horizon culture. If FINN-WILSON. lands one NZ anchor client, this is the shortlist.
nodes.warm_path: NZ network plus the relocation story.
nodes.first_move: Build the file now — sites, fleet mix, stated targets. Contact six months pre-move.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: Heavy Dutch e-truck adopter. The shipper-side perspective sharpens your operator story.
nodes.warm_path: NL events; possibly a Prologis tenant.
nodes.first_move: Ambient until launch.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: Public-facing NZ fleet electrifier, procurement-driven. Good ecosystem signal; slower commercial route.
nodes.first_move: Ambient; meet at NZ industry events after the move.

nodes.name: Anja van Niersen
nodes.why: The apex of your alumni network. Milence took €111M of AFIF funding in 2025 for truck charging parks including megawatt charging — the public-charging counterpart to your depot thesis.
nodes.first_move: One note this month: congratulate on the AFIF award, attach one depot-side observation she would find genuinely useful.

nodes.name: Former colleagues
nodes.why: The cheapest network maintenance available: people who already rate you, now scattered across the European charging ecosystem.
nodes.first_move: List them this week. One reactivation per fortnight, logged below.

nodes.name: Delta Charge
nodes.why: Your live BESS counterpart. The relationship exists; the depth doesn't yet. Their model — fixed-price charging on grid-connected batteries, backed by Delta Capacity — is your masterclass in structure.
nodes.first_move: Ask to walk the financial model and SPV structure. Frame it as making you a better delivery partner — which is true.

nodes.name: Steven Meersman
nodes.why: Built the company FINN-WILSON.'s endgame is modelled on: advisory-adjacent origins, financed-service platform, ANZ presence, gigawatt-scale storage.
nodes.warm_path: Via Gareth Ridge (ANZ) or UK industry events.
nodes.first_move: Long game. Engage the published thinking; aim for one 2027 conversation about how the early deals were structured.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: Your BP alumni route back into one of Europe's biggest truck-corridor builders.
nodes.first_move: One reactivation note to your strongest ex-BP contact; ask who runs fleet now.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: Dutch-built heavy charging technology, now inside Siemens. Hardware-side intelligence and a local network hub.
nodes.first_move: Meet at a Dutch industry event this year.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: The Dutch grid-and-charging brain trust. Association adds grid credibility to the brand — the dimension operators respect most.
nodes.first_move: Attend one ElaadNL session; cite their work in a post.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: Listed Dutch charging and storage manufacturer. Ecosystem awareness more than direct value.
nodes.first_move: Ambient.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: €600M debt financing in 2025 — proof that institutional lenders now bank charging at scale. A useful comparator for your capital-stack narrative.
nodes.first_move: Ambient; reference the financing in capital-stack content.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: Utility-scale entrant (DRIVE-E consortium, €45M AFIF). Utilities will sit as partner or competitor in every depot deal you ever do.
nodes.first_move: Ambient.

nodes.name: Gareth Ridge
nodes.why: Runs the most advanced fleet-electrification platform in your future market: 172 Auckland e-buses with Ritchies, six new depots by August 2026, Melbourne's largest e-bus depot, the Woolworths truck hub. Partner, benchmark, and the single most useful person to know before landing.
nodes.warm_path: European Zenobē orbit plus direct — your European depot record is the credential.
nodes.first_move: Direct note within two months: European operator relocating to NZ, one specific observation on the Ritchies rollout, ask for 20 minutes.

nodes.name: Richard Briggs
nodes.why: Runs delivery on the Low Emissions Heavy Vehicle Fund — 120 electric heavy vehicles co-funded so far, and publicly candid that uptake is slower than hoped. He needs exactly what you carry: deployment experience at scale.
nodes.warm_path: Cold but receptive — public role, public appetite for what works.
nodes.first_move: Share one European depot insight relevant to LEHVF design. Public servants remember useful outsiders.

nodes.name: Darren Miller
nodes.why: Funded the TGE trial and Zenobē's depots; ARENA's knowledge bank is your Australian homework. Visibility with ARENA is visibility with the entire serious end of the AU market.
nodes.first_move: Cite ARENA reports in two published pieces; attend one ARENA event before the move.

nodes.name: Tim Washington
nodes.why: Australia's fleet-charging pioneer, deep in the AU policy and OEM web. The fastest map of who matters in Australian e-mobility.
nodes.warm_path: ANZ e-mobility events; heavily mutual ecosystem.
nodes.first_move: Engage his commentary now; meet on the first Australian trip.

nodes.name: Calum Haslop
nodes.why: Largest ANZ bus operator and one of NZ's earliest e-fleet adopters. Bus depots taught this market how depot charging works — the operational history you want first-hand.
nodes.first_move: Verify role; connect via Gareth Ridge when NZ plans firm up.

nodes.name: Dean Roscherr
nodes.why: NZ's EV advocacy hub: live submissions on heavy-EV road user charge exemptions and accelerated depreciation for e-trucks. The room where NZ fleet policy gets shaped.
nodes.first_move: Join the orbit pre-move; offer European data for one submission.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: Sets the agency's direction. Matters at MOU level, not first-contact level.
nodes.first_move: Later.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: NZ's hydrogen heavy-transport play with a refuelling network. Competitor-adjacent intelligence: where hydrogen wins, and where your electrons win.
nodes.first_move: Ambient; understand their refuelling economics.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: The fastest route to fifty NZ corporate sustainability leads in one room.
nodes.first_move: Join in the first quarter on the ground; offer one talk.

nodes.name: Role seat
nodes.why: Heavy-duty public infrastructure operators. Long sales cycles, big tickets, strong reference value.
nodes.first_move: Ambient until established.

nodes.name: Kristofer Fröjd
nodes.why: Senior advisor at Decade Energy (€22M raised Apr 2026, zero-CapEx depot energy infra, ex-Volta Trucks team). Ex-strategy chief at Ellevio: grid flexibility depth. Live Decade-Prologis collab. Approached Finn after Hive Power webinar.
nodes.warm_path: Inbound. Live collab through Decade Energy x Prologis.
nodes.first_move: Substantive reply on BESS revenue stacking; propose standing exchange.
notes: 2026-06-12: deep dive done. Triple node: deal-adjacent counterpart, grid-flexibility educator, Stockholm investor-advisor.

nodes.name: Merlijn Borst
nodes.why: 56-message relationship. Tracked Finn's promotion, proposed June meeting.
nodes.first_move: Meeting locked in via other channel 2026-06-12.

nodes.name: Roland Steinmetz
nodes.why: Ex-Prologis energy/EV charging supplier. Amsterdam, senior, e-mobility strategy consultant.
nodes.first_move: Replied 2026-06-12; proposed Amsterdam coffee.

nodes.name: Steve Forster
nodes.role: County Councillor, Exec Member Highways & Transport
nodes.why: Past discussions on EV hubs at Prologis UK sites; wants to re-engage. Day-job commercial.
nodes.first_move: Replied 2026-06-12 asking which locations.

nodes.name: James Moat
nodes.why: UK CPO founder; eHGV fleet trials; asked where Prologis fits in ecosystem.
nodes.first_move: Replied 2026-06-12; call in coming weeks.

nodes.name: Philip Valarino
nodes.why: Green finance C-suite, ex-Macquarie (same house as Fleete/GIG). Knew Prologis from Macquarie days.
nodes.first_move: Replied 2026-06-12; meeting proposed re NEOT.

nodes.name: Matthijs van den Heuvel
nodes.why: NL heavy-duty charging ecosystem; asked whether Prologis opens DC networks to supplier trucks.
nodes.first_move: Reply pending in queue.

nodes.name: Junaid Faruq
nodes.why: Mid-conversation with Karen Peck (Prologis) before she left; wants to continue with Finn.
nodes.first_move: Reply pending; read Karen Peck context first.

nodes.name: Remco van Rookhuizen
nodes.why: 3-message thread; asked for virtual coffee (date passed).
nodes.first_move: Re-book coffee; reply pending.

nodes.name: Irina Kobera
nodes.why: Milence alumni network; warm note about Finn's new role.
nodes.first_move: Reactivation reply pending.

nodes.name: Rob Byrne
nodes.why: 7-message thread; collaboration chat requested.
nodes.first_move: Reply pending.

nodes.name: Liam Callaghan
nodes.why: Call happened Nov 2025; five follow-ups since, unanswered. Close or book.
nodes.first_move: Read thread; decide close vs call.

nodes.name: Sarah Papamichalis
nodes.why: 4-message thread; market intel on Shell Recharge divestment fallout.
nodes.first_move: Reply pending; intel is insight-graph evidence.

nodes.name: Armin Khoshniat
nodes.why: Asked about E.ON collaboration continuity; commercial excellence energy/e-mobility.
nodes.first_move: Reply pending.

nodes.name: Manuel Fernandes
nodes.why: Sees Prologis as 'hidden powerful CPO' for heavy-duty logistics transition; wants to speak.
nodes.first_move: Reply pending.

nodes.name: Luis Beires
nodes.why: Already inside the Prologis ecosystem (Brazil).
nodes.first_move: Reply pending.

nodes.name: Mohamed Mufid Hakim
nodes.why: 3-message thread; synergies pitch but credible background.
nodes.first_move: Reply pending.

nodes.name: Viktors Nikolajevs
nodes.why: Asked for brief call; CPO/FM/car-park focus.
nodes.first_move: Reply pending.

nodes.name: Pablo Decelis
nodes.why: Invited Finn to Green Mobility Exchange; events are map strategy (2-3 speaking slots/yr).
nodes.first_move: Assess event; reply pending.

nodes.name: Teng Chen
nodes.why: Offered help on C&I BESS; 3-message thread.
nodes.first_move: Reply pending.

nodes.name: Karel Misek
nodes.why: NZ-linked contact; ANZ bridge relevance.
nodes.first_move: Reply pending.

nodes.name: (TU Delft PhD - name in inbox)
nodes.why: Deep engagement with Finn's portfolio-energy content; content flywheel + research angle.
nodes.first_move: Identify in inbox; reply pending.

nodes.name: Allen Dong
nodes.why: Asked for intro brief of Finn's business.
nodes.first_move: One-liner reply pending.

nodes.name: Jonathon Fite
nodes.why: Chris-introduced contact; student tour request for May missed. Apology owed.
nodes.first_move: Short apology note.

edges.rationale: Decade Energy x Prologis live collaboration; Fröjd senior advisor at Decade.
Decade Energy and Delta Charge are both zero/low-CapEx depot BESS+charging platforms. Compare structures.
Valarino ex-Macquarie; Fleete is Macquarie GIG. Possible route to Chris Morrison.
evyve and Fleete both UK charging networks; market triangulation.
EVC previously supported Prologis on energy + EV charging.
NZ-linked contact; ANZ bridge ambient lane.
Shell Recharge divestment intel informs CPO-market read relevant to BP pulse tier.
UK public-charging-on-logistics-land conversations generalise to UK landlords.
Delta Capacity is the capital behind Delta Charge (from map).
Zenobē ANZ runs Woolworths Mascot hub (from map).
Kinetic in the Zenobē orbit (from map).
Zenobē co-founder and ANZ country director.
NZGIF funded Zenobē NZ entry (from map).
